label: Combat OS — Tactical Operator (UFC Gym Dublin)
description: A minimalist strength-led programme for a lean, advanced striker (30yo, 67kg) training around 3 weekly fight sessions. Priorities: relative strength, then conditioning/skill (covered by fight days), then explosiveness. Longevity-first: RPE-capped loads, no grinding, single-leg and anti-rotation work for joint health, no deadlift. Full-body x3 so a missed day never skips a movement pattern. PHASE 2 of the plan — graduate into this after the Foundation block (combatos-foundation-2026).

days.label: Day 1 — S&C: Lower + Posterior
days.focus: Squat strength, hip power, single-leg balance

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.cue: Explosive drive out of the hole; leave ~2 reps in reserve. This is your signature lift — keep every rep pristine. Load is secondary to bar speed and depth.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Machine (kickback)
exercises.cue: Your preferred glute machine — elbows supported, grab the handles, full extension and a hard squeeze. No hip-thrust setup at this gym, and this single-leg variant is better anyway: it's your no-deadlift hip-extension anchor (punch/kick power) AND builds the pelvic stability that protects your back.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat (DB)
exercises.cue: Own the balance before adding load. Directly corrects left/right asymmetry — fighting is a single-leg-dominant sport, so stance-leg strength matters more than bilateral.

exercises.name: Rotational Med-Ball Throw (astro zone)
exercises.cue: Max intent, hip-to-fist sequencing. This is POWER not conditioning — stop the set the moment speed drops. Trains the rotational output the weights floor neglects.

days.label: Day 2 — Fight
days.focus: Class / bag / sparring — log freely in notes

days.label: Day 3 — S&C: Push + Vertical Pull
days.focus: Overhead strength, vertical pull, shoulder health

exercises.name: Weighted Pull-Up (DB between feet)
exercises.cue: Hold a DB between the ankles for load — no dip belt at this gym yet. Full dead-hang to chin-over. If the DB is fiddly, swap to a heavy plate-loaded lat pulldown (your call in-app).

exercises.name: Barbell Overhead Press
exercises.cue: Brace hard, glutes tight, press to a stacked lockout over the crown. Your OHP has the most headroom of any lift — build it and your overhead integrity comes with it.

exercises.name: Incline DB Press
exercises.cue: Controlled descent, full stretch at the bottom. Balances the vertical pressing and keeps the shoulder girdle healthy under a striker's volume.

exercises.name: Cable Face Pull
exercises.cue: High elbows, pull to eye level, pause at the back. The single best insurance policy for striker shoulders — never skip it, never rush it.

days.label: Day 4 — Fight
days.focus: Class / bag / sparring — log freely in notes

days.label: Day 5 — S&C: Pull + Power + Core
days.focus: Horizontal pull, linear power, anti-rotation trunk

exercises.name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
exercises.cue: Flat back, pull to the sternum, explosive concentric. Balances all your pressing and builds the upper back that holds posture in the clinch.

exercises.name: Reverse Lunge (DB)
exercises.cue: Step back under control, drive through the front heel. A different single-leg stimulus from Day 1's Bulgarians — more athletic, less static.

exercises.name: Slam Ball (astro zone)
exercises.cue: Full overhead reach to a violent floor slam. Linear total-body power — quality reps only, reset and breathe between each. Astro zone.

exercises.name: Pallof Press (cable)
exercises.cue: Resist the rotation, don't create it. Anti-rotation trunk strength transfers to punch power and protects the spine far better than loaded crunches.

days.label: Day 6 — Fight
days.focus: Class / bag / sparring — log freely in notes

days.label: Day 7 — Pick / Recovery
days.focus: Your choice: recovery-studio session (cryo / infrared sauna / plunge), a yoga or mobility class, or a bonus session. Log what you did.
